documentation: [![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/photo_grid.svg?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://pypi.org/project/photo-grid/) [![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/photo_grid.svg?label=pypi%20downloads&logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://pypi.org/project/photo-grid/) [![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/photo_grid.svg?label=pypi%20version&logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://pypi.org/project/photo-grid/) [![](https://api.codacy.com/project/badge/Grade/626008b19df543ecb33a78e8f82f5e91)](https://app.codacy.com/manual/Poissonfish/photo_grid/dashboard) [![](https://img.shields.io/github/license/poissonfish/photo_grid)](https://github.com/Poissonfish/GRID/blob/master/LICENSE) [![](https://img.shields.io/github/languages/code-size/poissonfish/photo_grid)](https://github.com/Poissonfish/GRID/search?l=Python) ### [Software Page (zzlab.net)](https://zzlab.net/GRID) ### [User Manual](https://poissonfish.github.io/GRID/index.html) ## Get Started ### Installation (All users) ***Highly recommended install GRID in [Conda](https://poissonfish.github.io/GRID/ch1_started/installation.html) environment*** ```bash conda create -n grid python==3.11 conda install gdal conda install rasterio python -m pip install photo-grid ``` ### Launch GRID Just type `GRID` (case-sensitive) in your terminal. ```GRID``` or ```python -m grid```

functions.name: grid.lib.sortPts
documentation: NOTE: qmap has inverse Y! UP has smaller y value

functions.name: grid.lib.cropImg
documentation:
Perspectively project assigned area (pts) to a rectangle image
-----
param.
-----
img: 2-d numpy array
pts: a vector of xy coordinate, length is 4. Must be in the order as:
     (NW, NE, SE, SW)

functions.name: grid.lib.euclidean
documentation:
calculate Euclidean distance betweeen p1 and p2
input can be either tuple or matrix

functions.name: grid.lib.pickleGRID
documentation: Rotate a point counterclockwise by a given angle around a given origin.
The angle should be given in radians.
qx = ox + np.cos(ag) * (px - ox) - np.sin(ag) * (py - oy)
qy = oy + np.sin(ag) * (px - ox) + np.cos(ag) * (py - oy)

functions.name: grid.lib.invAffine
documentation:
convert GIS coordinate to (x, y)

NOTE:
# a = width of a pixel
# b = row rotation(typically zero)
# c = x-coordinate of the center of the upper-left pixel
# d = column rotation(typically zero)
# e = height of a pixel(typically negative)
# f = y-coordinate of the center of the upper-left pixel

functions.name: grid.lib.recover_scale
documentation:
recover the cropped shaped into original scale

parameters
----------
mat_in: 4 x 2 matrix
mat_H: 3 x 3 matrix
